messages.content:
Savey said on 01 December 2023

It appears the BBC One Christmas idents this year are focusing on CBBC show KikoRiki, Good considering the characters in that show are anthropomorphic animals, making them awesome for this time of year, I'd assume they will launch tonight in the junction between Doctor Who and Unbreakable, Doctor Who is their highest rating show, so it makes sense to lead into Unbreakable by launching the Christmas idents, Or they could hold it off till tommorow and launch them at Strictly. Whatever works.

Currently still running with the normal dancers idents (when will they be retired, they've been used since 2002)

Emma said on 01 December 2023

Hopefully S4C does something with their new blob mascot, Last year they had TV sets stacked up on top of each other, Then for 10 months they had flickering TV sets, They have finally rebranded and have introduced their first ever mascot: a shape-shifting blob, I hope BBC One rebrand soon, those dancers sure are getting tired, As for the KikoRiki themed Christmas idents, I was told they are launching tonight after Doctor Who, They are being introduced just before Unbreakable.

HouseofFun said on 01 December 2023

Doctor Who ending now, announcement playing "A team of 8 people are pushed to the absolute limit, Unbreakable, our brand new game show is up next, But first, Time to say hello and spend Christmas with the Doctor" I'm not liking this one a whole lot..., They seem to be running a trailer advertising the Doctor Who Christmas special, which revolves around the Doctor having to protect his companions from a man who seeks to find a fob watch (the one The Doctor made himself human with one time), Although just before Doctor Who tonight they had a announcement saying "Christmas is switched on tonight in 1 hour in time for Unbreakable, But first, Is this the end for the Doctor?". Doesn't seem like they are advertising the Christmas pres at all, 

Edit: Christmas pres featuring KikoRiki switched on, First ident shown features Rosa the Pig doing a dramatic ice skate dance (She even uses eyedrops for fake tears) While this is happening we hear dramatic music, The announcer says "Welcome to Christmas on BBC One, Let us entertain you, But now a team of 8 people are being pushed to their absolute limits, Are they Unbreakable?" Not sure why they didn't tell us it was happening untill it did, But still a gorgeous ident, I noticed when the Announcer started talking it sounded as if he was driven to tears by the beauty of Rosa's gorgeous dance. The best thing to come out of this package since the "Lights" christmas idents in 2006, 

Eyes said on 01 December 2023

A beautiful ident, Best thing to come out of the "Rythm and Movement" package since those Planet Earth 3 special idents a few months ago, i feel sad that KikoRiki has not been shown much on CBBC on BBC Two, but I'm glad it's representing the Xmas idents on BBC One (Although I also wish the idents also featured a merman).

Maile said on 01 December 2023

Best Christmas ident in a while, I just love that they have spent it with Rosa and her friends, I hear there is also one featuring Krash the Rabbit opening his presents, I like how they used the usual ident theme (dancing) in that first ident, KikoRiki looks like it's gonna be good this year, but I hope they do more with the style (and maybe finally retire Rythm and Movement idents)

Faramad said on 01 December 2023

News safe ident just on, Basically just a Christmas tree on a snowy background, with a music box Christmas carol playing, on top of that the only thing we see is a sleeping Rosa (We vaguely hear her snoring) Glad they are now using characters in their news safe idents, Announcer said "Now on BBC One, time for the BBC News at 8". Remember the Lights idents the used just before overhauling their promo style in late 2006/early 2007? That was pretty cool, but this is way better. I seem to remember those idents started on a dark street but Christmas lights started turning on before it got to a city center and then people were dancing, It was used again in 2007, And now it seems like the "Project Chameleon" endboard template introduced in July is out the window, at least for now, replaced with a snowy background with a Christmas tree on it. Signs that Rythm and Movement is finally working towards the end.

KikoRiki said on 01 December 2023

We're glad you love our cartoon and the special BBC One idents featuring our characters, 

However, Rosa will be the star, (We will have idents revolving around Chiko, Krash and the others), As to what you said, BBC One are looking into introducing the Circle idents already in use in other countries, so don't expect to see Ballet, Capoeria or Festival/Rave back in the new year. Get used to Kites, Capes, Hippos and Bikes as that's what might be coming after our Christmas campaign, We don't know for certain, but the Dancers might not be coming back after Christmas

WhovianForever: Did everyone catch the Doctor Who Christmas special announcement during the news? I’m so hyped! Can’t wait to see how the Doctor deals with the fob watch saga this holiday season!

LemonyFan: And how about they’re showing the 2004 ‘A Series of Unfortunate Events’ movie!! That’s one of my holiday favorites; the nostalgia is real. 😍🍿

ChristmasCarol: Truly is shaping up to be a wonderful holiday schedule on BBC One. Between Doctor Who and the classic movies, I’m going to be glued to my screen with all the mulled wine and mince pies!

KikoFanatic: Plus, the KikoRiki Christmas idents have been adorable so far! Rosa’s ice skate dance nearly brought tears to my eyes just like the announcer. 🐷⛸ Christmas is definitely switched on!

RetroTV: I’m just here wondering if the new ‘Circle’ idents KikoRiki hinted at is a sign we’re finally saying goodbye to the “Rythm and Movement” package for good. After 20+ years, it’s about time for a major refresh!

NostalgiaNerd: @RetroTV I’m with you on that. The Lights idents back in '06/'07 were beautiful. It’s cool to see they’re still willing to shake things up visually with the news safe ident this year. Sleepy Rosa might just be my new favorite. 😴🎄

SeriesBinger: Doctor Who special + A Series of Unfortunate Events + KikoRiki idents = Best Christmas TV lineup or what? Also, hope BBC One throws in some classic Christmas episodes of our favorite shows. Maybe some ‘Vicar of Dibley’ or ‘Only Fools and Horses’?

CircleCurious: Anyone heard more about these new Circle idents? I love change, and KikoRiki characters have been great, but I’m already excited for what’s coming next. Bring on the new era!

KikoLover: Just saw a new Krash and Chiko ident! They’re having a blast playing in the snow! ❄️🐇 So cute watching Krash throw snowballs while Chiko dodges around. Really captures the winter fun vibes.

SciFiSavvy: Speaking of new idents, did anyone else notice the Dokko the Moose building a laser device ident? It aired right before ‘Doctor Who’ and set the PERFECT tone for sci-fi. Dokko’s always been the tech genius of the group. 🦌🔧

HedgehogAdmirer: @KikoLover Chiko is always adorable, but seeing him in the snow was next level! BBC One knows how to do Christmas idents right. Plus, the animation on these characters in the idents is super impressive this year.

WhovianGuru: @SciFiSavvy Loved the Dokko ident! It’s like a mini episode of ‘Doctor Who’ in itself. Brilliant tie-in to the show’s theme! Makes waiting for the Christmas special even more exciting.

BakeMaster: But guys, let’s talk about the Olga the Owl baking ident. The detail on that gingerbread house she was making! I can almost smell the cookies through the screen. 🦉🍪 It’s the cozy ident I never knew I needed in my life.

HolidayWatcher: Right?! The Olga ident feels like being wrapped in a warm blanket with a hot chocolate in hand. It’s chef’s kiss perfection. BBC One’s going all out with these KikoRiki characters for Christmas.

DoctorFan: Dokko’s ident was like a mini teaser for what’s coming with ‘Doctor Who’. Immersive and full of that tech mystery vibe. I appreciate that BBC One creates these unique intros for thematic continuity.

AnimationAppreciator: Major kudos to the animation team for the KikoRiki idents. As someone who dabbles in animation, the quality is sensational. It’s like a little holiday gift before each show starts!

ChristmasCheer: Can’t get enough of the snow play with Krash and Chiko. It’s absolutely delightful and spreads so much joy. These little moments provided by the idents add a special touch to the holiday season.

SciCompanion: Dokko and his inventions always remind me that anything’s possible with a little imagination. Such an inspiring ident to lead into ‘Doctor Who’. Well played, BBC One! Can’t wait to see what else is in store, I'm gonna miss these idents when new year comes around! 😢

WelcomeToSciFi: The Dokko centric ident really sets the scene for what's to come on ‘Doctor Who’ this Christmas. One of the best things about the KikoRiki set, Far better than the usual dancers, Come to think of it, they have been using the dancers since 2002, so now is a good time for a revamp, I guess 😉

Olgo5: Just saw the Olga ident and damn, Olga really is a talented chef, They always seem to show it before cooking shows, Which ident normally leads into Eastenders, It seems to vary between Krash and Chiko in the snow, Olga cooking and Rosa ice skating. Not sure how this is consistent, But that Rosa one brings  tears to my eyes with her graceful skating, Not sure why the Dokko one only plays before Doctor Who, Maybe because it fits it well

Prolouge:

User1: So, what’s everyone thinking BBC One’s going to pull out of the hat for this year’s Christmas idents? Last year was decent, but I’m hoping for something with a bit more pizzazz.

WhovianForever: I’m crossing my fingers for something Doctor Who-themed! With David Tennant back in the show earlier this year, it would be perfect timing to capitalize on that for the festive season. Can you imagine an ident with the TARDIS all decked out in Christmas lights?

HolidayJoy: That would be cool for us Whovians, for sure, but BBC One usually goes for something that appeals to everyone. Doctor Who is big, but not universally loved by every single viewer. Plus, they tend to lean towards more generic festive symbols.

SeriesBinger: True, they’ll probably aim for something more inclusive. Like a cozy winter scene or playful animation. Maybe bring back some classic characters or even partner with a popular children’s show this time around for wider appeal?

RetroTV: I’ve got a hunch they’ll go with animation this year. It has that universal charm, right? Plus, it would work perfectly with both the kids’ content and the family-friendly vibe they love during Christmas.

User1: That’s an interesting angle. They could get quite creative with animation. Plus, they’re probably due for a change since the “Rythm and Movement” dancers have been around since 2002.

KikoFanatic: What if they teamed up with a show like KikoRiki? The characters are fun, visually engaging, and it’s something that would stand out. Plus, they could do loads with those quirky little animals.

LemonyFan: KikoRiki would be such a cute choice! It could be a hit with children and adults alike if done right. And it’s refreshingly different from usual idents, without relying on something niche like a specific Doctor Who theme.

WhovianForever: Okay, I see your point. Much as I’d love Doctor Who idents, something like KikoRiki could offer that ‘Christmas with the family’ vibe they probably want. I just hope they add a little extra magic this year, you know?

CircleCurious: Whatever they do, I hope it’s something new. We’ve had the “Rythm and Movement” package idents for such a long time. It’s high time for something fresh and exciting to take us into the new year! Write a forum convo in early Jaunary where the Xmas idents persist, although promo presentation is back to normal, the Xmas idents will stay until January 9th

User1: Happy New Year, folks! Did everyone see that BBC One is keeping the KikoRiki Christmas idents until January 9th? Quite a festive extension!

WhovianForever: Yeah, noticed that. They’re really squeezing every last drop out of the festive season. Not complaining, though—the idents are adorable.

HolidayJoy: I'm loving it! January always feels a bit dreary, so hanging onto a bit of Christmas cheer is fine by me. Plus, these KikoRiki idents have brought a much-needed fresh vibe.

SeriesBinger: It's funny how we're back to the standard promos but still ending on a Christmassy note with the idents. Feels like wearing a Santa hat to a regular workday. 😂

RetroTV: Probably for the best. A gradual transition is less jarring than going cold turkey from festive to normal overnight. Those dancers did overstay their welcome, so I'm not complaining about a little extra time with the KikoRiki crew.

KikoFanatic: More time with the KikoRiki characters? Yes, please! 🐰❄️ They should do character idents more often, not just for Christmas. Imagine seasonal idents throughout the year!

LemonyFan: That’s a lovely idea, KikoFanatic! Seasonal idents would keep things fresh and give us something new to look forward to every few months.

CircleCurious: Agreed, it would keep BBC One visually exciting. About time they embrace change more regularly. By the way, has anyone else spotted the new promo endboards? What happened to the "Project Chameleon" layout?

SciFiSavvy: Yeah, those new endboards slipped right under the radar with all the festive fuss. It looks like they're definitely moving away from the Chameleon style. Maybe setting up for a bigger overhaul soon?

AnimationAppreciator: Gotta give credit to the animation team for their work on these idents. Sticking around or not, they've set a high bar for whatever comes next. Here's hoping BBC One keeps up this level of quality!

ChristmasCheer: It's definitely brightening up my January evenings. Although, I must say, I’m starting to miss the standard continuity. It makes me wonder what they have in store for us after the 9th.

WhovianGuru: Side note, has anyone considered that maybe these extended idents are a test run? Sort of gauging viewer reactions for future changes. The response seems pretty positive, after all!

HolidayWatcher: Possibly! It would be smart for BBC One to use this as a barometer for the audience's appetite for innovative idents. Positive feedback could mean more creative risks in the future.

DoctorFan: Still, I can’t help but wonder what’s next. Are the Circle idents coming for sure? Will the KikoRiki influence the new standard? It’s all very intriguing!

BakeMaster: Those KikoRiki idents are a tough act to follow. I hope they continue with the charming and creative streak. As a baking fan, I really appreciated Olga’s little cooking sequences.

HedgehogAdmirer: You and me both, BakeMaster. After this, plain old idents will feel like a letdown. I say embrace the characters and creativity, and give us something that makes us smile like the Christmas ones did!

SciCompanion: Couldn't agree more. The Dokko ident was just stellar. Any ident that can create a little story around it will always trump static dancers for me. Here’s to whatever imaginative world BBC One transports us to next! 🌌📺
Write a tweet after the 9th, about the idents in use afterwards

Just caught the latest #BBCOne idents post-Christmas and wow, they really are embracing change! Out with the old and in with the new – loving the fresh, vibrant look. Will miss the #KikoRiki charm, but excited to see what creative directions they'll explore this year. 📺✨ #NewEraBBC
Write a forum conversation of the rebrand launch, and they notice the idents have a 5 note jingle

User1: Did anyone else catch the launch of the new BBC One idents last night? I was so impressed with the crisp look! And that catchy 5-note jingle is already stuck in my head. 🎶📺✨

TVJunkie: Totally saw that, User1! The rebrand feels modern and energetic. Loving the streamlined design. And yes, those 5 notes are like the audio signature of the channel now.

VisualVibes: Agreed on the jingle, it’s subtle yet memorable. The whole package just pops. The idents have this dynamic quality that's a stark (and welcome) departure from what we're used to.

MelodyMaker: As a music buff, I appreciate when networks use a distinctive sound like this. It’s like BBC One saying "hello" in its own language every time an ident plays. Super smart branding.

ArtWatcher: It's not just the sound, the visual flow of the idents is so pleasing. Each one tells a small story, very much like a vignette. Plus, they work perfectly across various devices – great for the streaming age.

CircleCurious: Honestly, it's a breath of fresh air after those “Rythm and Movement” idents that lasted over two decades. This feels like BBC One finally stepping confidently into the 2020s.

NostalgiaNerd: I must admit, I’m a bit nostalgic for the old idents already, but this change was needed. The new jingle and contemporary look give BBC One a fresh personality.

RebrandFan: Seriously, this rebrand is slick. I noticed each ident ties into the next program seamlessly. And that jingle – it’s like the auditory version of a logo. Instantly recognizable.

SoundWave: Anyone know who composed the jingle? It's got the same earworm quality as the Intel bongs or the Netflix 'ta-dum'. Curious who we have to thank for our latest last-song syndrome.

GraphicGuru: That would be interesting to find out, SoundWave. On another note, the color scheme and animation style they're using are on-point for 2023. BBC One is looking fresh to death!

TechieTalk: Can we talk about how these idents are optimized for different screen types and sizes? Smart move with consumption so varied these days. Auditory and visual cohesion across platforms is the future.

IdentsEnthusiast: For me, the true test was the news transition ident – and they nailed it. Still dignified but with that new twist. And that jingle – simple, effective, timeless. Here’s to new traditions!

KikoLover: The new idents are great, but I confess, I’ll miss the KikoRiki Christmas charm. Glad I recorded some of those idents for keepsake. 😅 This rebrand does make the channel feel more 21st century, though.

SciFiSavvy: Watched a sci-fi themed ident right before Doctor Who, and it fit the mood perfectly. That 5-note jingle before the TARDIS whirr – chills! BBC One’s idents game is solid.

ChannelHopper: Quick question, are the idents different depending on the time of day? Thought I noticed subtler tones in the evening. If they're tailoring them like that, it's another layer of detail I'm here for.

BrandingExpert: ChannelHopper, good catch. Many networks use a dayparting strategy for their branding. It sets the mood for viewing habits throughout the day. BBC One seems to do it with finesse—those jingles are like a cherry on top.

Write a blog post saying the new idents revolve around circles and people coming together, Examples include kites flying in a circle, hippos swimming in a circle, bikes riding in a circle, and a moon rover moving in a circle, The transition began in early December, and slowly but surely, elements of the 2002 look started to dissappear

Title: BBC One’s New Idents Celebrate Togetherness in Circles

After nearly two decades of familiar "Rythm and Movement" dancers gracing our screens, BBC One has unveiled a new set of idents that revolve—quite literally—around the theme of circles and unity. This significant visual pivot began subtly in early December, but as the crescendo of change built, by the New Year, all elements of the 2002 look had vanished, leaving room for a fresh, contemporary identity.

The new idents can be described as a celebration of togetherness, with each sequence artfully choreographed around circular motions that hint at inclusivity and community. The recurring theme of circles not only aesthetically ties the idents together but also serves as a metaphor for continuity and connection among people, nature, and technology.

One of the most visually striking idents features a group of colorful kites dancing in the sky, their tails and strings intertwining as they glide in a perfect circle. It’s a mesmerizing display that evokes childhood memories and outdoor gatherings—universal experiences that resonate with viewers from all walks of life.

Then there’s the whimsically charming ident with a pod of hippos, their bulky forms surprisingly graceful as they perform an almost balletic sequence in the water, encircling one another in a display of playful camaraderie. On a deeper level, it signifies the sheer joy of beings in their natural habitat, existing in harmony with the world around them.

For the more urban-oriented audience, an ident showcases a squad of cyclists navigating a city roundabout, their bikes forming a dynamic, rotating wheel—a nod to the bustling life of cities and the people that keep them turning.

Lastly, there’s a futuristic glimpse into exploration with a moon rover tracing a loop on the dusty lunar surface. Here the circle stands for humanity's boundless curiosity and shared ambition, the tracks we leave on new frontiers echoing the unity back on Earth.

Throughout the transition, which was executed with a delicate hand, viewers witnessed the phasing out of the old and the introduction of the new. What started with subtle changes—a gradual replacement of graphics, the integration of a new jingle, and the retirement of the iconic dancers—culminated in a fully realized rebranding. This wasn't just a swap of visuals; it was a considerate process reflective of BBC One's understanding of audience attachment and nostalgia.

With these new idents, BBC One has evolved its on-screen language, encapsulating the spirit of modern Britain while signaling its intent to remain a central point around which viewers can gather. The use of the circle is genius in its simplicity—a universal shape that holds infinite possibilities.

As we adjust to these new visuals and the delightful 5-note jingle accompanying each ident, one thing is clear: BBC One has taken a bold step toward defining its identity for the coming years, prioritizing connection and the collective experience that television can offer. The 2002 era may be behind us, but its foundational spirit of rhythm and movement lives on—reimagined through unity in circles.
Write a blog post about the interim look, which served to bridge the gap between R&M and C

Title: The Art of Transition: BBC One’s Interim Look Bridges the Past and Future

In the world of television branding, change is an art form—one that requires a delicate balance between honoring tradition and embracing innovation. When BBC One decided it was time to move on from its iconic "Rhythm & Movement" idents of 2002, the network faced the challenge of bridging the gap to a new era. The solution? An interim look that deftly served as the bridge, a respectful nod to the past while paving the way towards the 'Circle' idents of the future.

The interim period was a masterclass in subtlety and finesse. Long-time viewers of BBC One are a loyal bunch, many of whom grew up with the familiar silhouettes of dancers gracing their screens in various vibrant scenarios. The "Rhythm & Movement" idents had become more than just station identification; they were part of a communal memory, a piece of living room heritage.

Recognizing this, BBC One approached the transition with the care it deserved. Over the course of several months, changes were softly introduced. The dancers began to fade into the background, their appearances less frequent and more subdued. The colors and movement that once defined the idents were slowly replaced with more minimalist and abstract designs. It was as if the channel was cleaning its palette, preparing viewers for a new taste, all without causing a jarring visual departure that might come with an abrupt overhaul.

What made the interim look stand out was its strategic use of evocative graphics that retained a sense of fluid motion without being anchored to any specific form. This allowed for a gentle disassociation from the "Rhythm & Movement" idents and an adjustment period for the audience. There was a thoughtful softness to the transitional idents, often featuring natural elements like water or sky in gentle motion, offering a calming, contemplative visual experience.

A unique aspect of the interim look was its auditory transition. The 5-note jingle, which would eventually become synonymous with the 'Circle' idents, was teased, giving audiences an audible anchor that they would become more familiar with over time. It's a testament to BBC One's understanding of the importance of sound in branding—how a few simple notes can conjure up a sense of identity just as powerfully as any visual.

As the transition unfolded, the on-screen graphics and fonts were also updated. Though not yet the final 'Circle' design, these elements leaned more towards the simplicity and clarity that would characterize the new idents. The use of space, clean lines, and a more neutral color palette suggested modernity without alienating viewers who had come to associate BBC One with a more vibrant and diverse look.

BBC One's interim period is a case study in how to handle brand evolution with grace and respect for your audience. It's a reminder that while change is inevitable, it doesn't have to be confrontational. Instead, it can be an invitation—a gentle taking by the hand to lead loyal viewers towards exciting new horizons. As we now embrace the 'Circle' idents and all they symbolize, we'll remember the interim look fondly: the bridge that carried us over familiar waters and into new streams of creativity and connection.
Write a tweet where the dancers took their last bow on 1st of December, then talk about the final interim look, the Xmas one

Saying farewell to an era, the #BBCOne dancers took their last bow on Dec 1. 🕺💃 Now, the final interim look takes stage, marrying a touch of festive cheer with a glimpse of what's to come. The #Xmas idents sparkle with transition—the perfect encore to a beloved legacy. 🎄✨ #EndOfAnEra #NewBeginnings
Write a tweet saying the Xmas look is cute, and list a few

The #Xmas look on #BBCOne is absolutely adorable – cute hippos making snow angels, playful kites with festive tails, and even a moon rover drawing a giant snowflake! 🎅🚀 Love the festive vibes these charming idents bring! ❄️✨ #HolidaySpirit #ChristmasIdents
Write about the previous year's festive look, which began the beginning of the end for the dancers

Title: Reflecting on BBC One’s Yuletide Farewell to the Dancers

As snowflakes began to grace our screens and jingle bells resonated through our homes, BBC One embarked on a festive journey that would also mark the twilight of an era for its iconic 'Rhythm & Movement' idents. Last year's Christmas look was not just a seasonal celebration but an understated prelude to the final departure of the dancers that had become a visual staple of the network.

The festive look of the previous year was imbued with the comforting glow of tradition, yet there was a subtle signal amidst the merrymaking: change was on the horizon. The dancers that had been twirling, leaping, and bopping against vivid backdrops since 2002 were slowly taking their closing steps. The idents continued to sparkle with Christmas motifs, but there was a palpable sense of nostalgia as if each pirouette was a little more poignant, each silhouette a touch more reflective.

Viewers were treated to classic scenes of holiday cheer, with the dancers performing against seasonal settings that brought the warmth of Christmas to life. There was a keen attempt to marry the legacy of the past with the freshness of the present—a transitional nod that, while festive, hinted at the new chapter ready to unfold in the year ahead.

Notably, the inclusion of subtle yet modern elements—a softening of edges, a slightly muted color palette, and an introduction of newer, softer musical cues—spoke to the impending transition. These adjustments were artful and well-measured, painting a picture of continuity while sowing the seeds for the adoption of newer branding elements.

The Christmas idents of the previous year thus served as a gentle primer for audiences, easing them into an emotional preparedness for a future without their familiar dancers. It was a festive period that lovingly juxtaposed yesteryear's charm against the canvas of tomorrow, connecting memories of the past with anticipation for the future.

As we now look back, we understand those idents were a festive buffer, cushioning the inevitable leap into a new visual identity. They allowed viewers one last Christmas waltz with the figures that had become as much a part of BBC One's identity as the Christmas programming itself.

In bidding farewell to these silhouetted partners in dance, BBC One carefully curated a festive transitional look that served to honor the legacy of what came before while stepping confidently into the new. Last Christmas was a reminder of the many shared moments with the dancers, the finale of a beloved routine before the curtains rose for the next act—a beautifully orchestrated adieu.

Write the tweet when the curtains closed, and saying that for the next month, KikoRiki characters will grace our festive screens, list a few idents featuring them, and say the Circles launch on the 9th of Jan

As the curtains close on the #BBCOne dancers, we get ready to embrace a month of charming #KikoRiki characters bringing festive joy to our screens! 🌟 Look out for Rosa's ice dance, Krash's snowball fun, and Chiko's present unwrapping adventure! 🎁🐷❄️ The new #Circles era begins Jan 9th. Prepare to be dazzled! ✨ #ChristmasWithKikoRiki #NewYearNewLook
